title: elaeocarpus rumphii subsp. ternatensis
intro: Elaeocarpus rumphii subsp. ternatensis, also known as the Ternate Blue Olive or Ternate Blue Fig, is a plant species in the Elaeocarpus genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus rumphii subsp. ternatensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water or using its bark to create a decoction, and it is often consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions.
